mission.title: Install and Configure Atlantis on Kubernetes
mission.description: Atlantis is a self-hosted application that automates Terraform pull request workflows by running `terraform plan`, `import`, and `apply` commands in response to pull request events. Installing Atlantis allows teams to collaborate on Terraform changes more effectively and standardize their workflows.

steps.title: Add the Atlantis Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Atlantis Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add runatlantis https://runatlantis.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Atlantis using Helm
steps.description:
Install Atlantis in a new namespace called `atlantis` with the following command:
```bash
helm install atlantis runatlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis --create-namespace --version v0.40.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Atlantis pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace atlantis
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding to access Atlantis
steps.description:
To access the Atlantis UI, set up port forwarding with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/atlantis --namespace atlantis 4141:4141
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Atlantis will have the pods running in the `atlantis` namespace, and you will be able to access the Atlantis UI through port forwarding. You can further configure resource limits to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add runatlantis https://runatlantis.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install atlantis runatlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis --create-namespace --version v0.40.0

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Atlantis release using Helm to remove the deployment from the cluster. Run the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall atlantis --namespace atlantis
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Atlantis. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace atlantis
kubectl delete crd atlantis.*
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the `atlantis` namespace to clean up all remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace atlantis
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and any persistent data. You can export the current Helm release configuration with:
```bash
helm get values atlantis --namespace atlantis > atlantis-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Atlantis release to the latest version. Run the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade atlantis runatlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis --version v0.41.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Atlantis pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace atlantis
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Atlantis pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. Use the following command:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace atlantis
```
Look for any configuration errors or issues with resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to access Atlantis UI
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Atlantis UI via port forwarding, ensure that the service is running and the port is correctly forwarded. Check the service status with:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace atlantis
```
If the service is not running, verify the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Terraform plan fails
troubleshooting.description:
If Terraform plans are failing, check the Atlantis logs for specific error messages. Use:
```bash
kubectl logs <atlantis-pod-name> --namespace atlantis
```
Ensure that the necessary permissions and configurations are set for the Terraform workspace.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails, check for compatibility issues with the new version. Review the output of the upgrade command and check the current configuration with:
```bash
helm get values atlantis --namespace atlantis
```
You may need to rollback using:
```bash
helm rollback atlantis --namespace atlantis
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed to deploy Atlantis.
